title: Finite Perceptron

solver_reuse.target: finite perceptron bad weight-update QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact finite trace replay computes the first weight coordinate -1 after the second perceptron mistake update, while a malformed row claims 1, isolating a compact scalar Farkas conflict beneath the training trace.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional perceptron rows only when they add distinct averaged/voted-perceptron, kernel, margin-bound, non-separable, learning-rate, or numerical-honesty pressure; keep Novikoff mistake bounds, convergence theorems, and floating-point training claims in theorem or statistical-inference lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed finite training set, augmented coordinates, initial weights, every presented step's dot-product score, mistake flag, and weight update, the final weights, and the strict-margin convergence pass as deterministic exact-rational replay obligations.
Route the false weight-update claim through a source-linked QF_LRA artifact and checked Farkas evidence after exact replay computes the conflicting coordinate equation.
Expose the pack through statistics, probability, linear-algebra, perceptron, exact-vs-floating, and learner-query surfaces.

Keep Novikoff mistake bounds, perceptron convergence theorems, margin theory, non-separable and kernel behavior, learning-rate policy, statistical generalization, and floating-point training marked theorem, statistical-inference, or numerical-honesty horizon.
